name: Path to the top of Olta (Circular PR-CV 340)
name_original: Sendero a la cima d'Olta (Circular PR-CV 340)
region_intro: Hiking around Calp offers diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains to dramatic coastal cliffs. The region is characterized by its iconic limestone outcrop, Peñón de Ifach, and features serene natural parks. Pine forests and salt flats also contribute to the varied terrain available for exploration. These physical features provide a range of hiking experiences for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Calp The most popular hiking route is Ascent to Penyal d'Ifac — Circular from Calpe, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 49 minutes to complete. This route ascends the iconic limestone outcrop, offering panoramic views of the coastline and Mediterranean Sea. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route through the Parc Natural del Penyal d'Ifac, a difficult 5.2 miles (8.4 km) path. It explores the natural park, known for its unique flora and diverse fauna. Local hikers also love the Path to the top of Olta (Circular PR-CV 340), a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through pine forests, often completed in about 4 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Calp is defined by iconic rock formations, varied mountain ranges, and stunning coastal trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Calp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 2,100 reviews. More than 22,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Calp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Calp?
A: Calp offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 180 routes recorded on komoot. These include 104 easy routes, 60 moderate routes, and 22 difficult routes, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Calp?
A: Yes, Calp has numerous easy and moderate trails perfect for beginners and families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351357851/benissa-ecological-trail-and-inland-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benissa Ecological Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic coastal walk, while the circular path around the Calp Salt Flats is also a gentle option. For a slightly more challenging but still family-friendly experience, the Serra d'Oltà provides trails through pine forests with rewarding views.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Calp?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Calp are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Notable circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e867376960/ascent-to-penyal-d-ifac-circular-from-calpe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Penyal d'Ifac — Circular from Calpe&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e869924965/path-to-the-top-of-olta-circular-pr-cv-340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path to the top of Olta (Circular PR-CV 340)&lt;/a&gt;, and the walking trail around the Calp Salt Flats.

Q: What are the most iconic natural landmarks to see while hiking in Calp?
A: The most iconic landmark is undoubtedly the &lt;strong&gt;Peñón de Ifach&lt;/strong&gt;, a dramatic limestone outcrop offering panoramic views. Other impressive natural features include the cliffs of &lt;strong&gt;Morro de Toix&lt;/strong&gt;, providing stunning coastal vistas, and the unique natural rock tunnel known as the &lt;strong&gt;Forat&lt;/strong&gt; in the Sierra Bernia mountain range.

Q: Is it possible to spot wildlife on the trails around Calp?
A: Yes, Calp offers opportunities for wildlife spotting. The &lt;strong&gt;Calp Salt Flats&lt;/strong&gt; are a protected natural area, famous for diverse birdlife, including flamingos, especially during certain seasons. The &lt;strong&gt;Peñón de Ifach Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; is also home to unique flora, such as the *Silene Hifacensis*, and various fauna.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Calp?
A: For experienced hikers, the ascent to the summit of &lt;strong&gt;Peñón de Ifach&lt;/strong&gt; is considered difficult due to rocky and steep sections, but rewards with breathtaking views. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Bernia&lt;/strong&gt; mountain range also offers a more challenging circular trail with rugged terrain and significant inclines, including the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat de Bèrnia&lt;/a&gt; natural rock tunnel.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or caves to explore near Calp's hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region around Calp features interesting natural attractions. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; in the nearby gorges. For caves, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat de Bèrnia&lt;/a&gt; is a natural rock tunnel that hikers can pass through, and there are other caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2888413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Cendra&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4756985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Moraig - Cova dels Arcs&lt;/a&gt; to discover.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Calp?
A: The hiking routes in Calp are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 2,100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning panoramic views from iconic landmarks like Peñón de Ifach, the diverse landscapes ranging from coastal paths to pine forests, and the variety of trails suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any historical sites to discover along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, history is woven into the landscape around Calp. Along the Sierra Bernia trail, you can visit the ruins of the 16th-century &lt;strong&gt;Fort de Bernia&lt;/strong&gt;, which was built to defend against pirate attacks. The &lt;strong&gt;Calp Salt Flats&lt;/strong&gt; also have historical significance for salt production, and the &lt;strong&gt;Ermita Vella&lt;/strong&gt; (Old Hermitage) can be found on the Serra d'Oltà trails.

Q: What are some good options for coastal walks in Calp?
A: Calp boasts beautiful coastal ecological paths. The &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Ecológico de Benissa&lt;/strong&gt; (Benissa Ecological Trail) and the Ruta Voramar Borumbot offer scenic strolls with stunning views of the Mediterranean Sea and access to hidden coves, providing a relaxing yet picturesque hiking experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Calp?
A: The best time to hike in Calp is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, winter months. The landscapes are also particularly vibrant in spring.

Q: Are there any trails that offer unique geological features?
A: Beyond the iconic Peñón de Ifach, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Bernia&lt;/strong&gt; range is known for its rugged terrain and the unique natural rock tunnel called the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat&lt;/a&gt;. Passing through this tunnel leads to spectacular views of the surrounding valleys, making it a memorable geological highlight.
